target.name: Silicon Box
target.founder: Han Byung Joon, Sehat Sutardja, Weili Dai

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $200M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 20.6x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Positron AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series B), post-money $1B+

data.narrative:
Silicon Box is a Series B semiconductor company founded by Han Byung Joon, Sehat Sutardja, and Weili Dai, having raised $200M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of 8 private peers (including Positron AI, Etched, and d-Matrix) and 3 public semiconductor tickers (ALAB, NVDA, AMD).

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis uses the $200M total raised as a valuation proxy and applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement, yielding $380M. Peer-median analysis across 6 same-sector and same/adjacent-stage peers produces a median valuation of $1.0B, with a range spanning $500M to $5.0B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied due to lack of disclosed revenue data, which is standard for private companies. Public Semiconductors trade at a median 20.6x price-to-sales, but remains inaccessible without target financials.

The two applicable methods triangulate to a low of $323M, median of $690M, and high of $1.1B. The wide dispersion between stage-progression ($380M) and peer-median ($1.0B) reflects uncertainty inherent in early-stage semiconductor valuations, where funding quantum, capital efficiency, and addressable market positioning vary substantially across the peer set. Key uncertainties include revenue traction, product timeline clarity, and capital requirements to next milestone.
